title: Rome, Naples, Florence, Venice & Milan.

trains.name: Trains & Taxi Transfers
trains.blurb: Scheduled rail journeys linking five of Italy's most iconic cities, with private transfers at each end.

transport.1: Rome Airport to Rome hotel, private transfer.
transport.2: Rome - small group walking tour of Imperial Rome with the Colosseum, 2.5 hrs.
transport.3: Rome to Naples train ticket, II Class.

private.name: Private Exclusive Coach
private.blurb: A private coach and driver at your disposal for the whole journey, with guided tours in Rome and Venice.

transport.1: Rome Airport to Rome hotel, private transfer. Private coach at your disposal from Day 1 until Day 11.
transport.4: Guided city tour, 3 hrs, with your private vehicle (no entrances included).
transport.8: Venice - private guided walking tour, 1.5 hrs.

inclusions: Private coach at your disposal from Day 1 until Day 11
Rome airport to hotel private transfer on arrival

days.title: Arrive Rome
days.desc: Arrive in the Eternal City and transfer to your hotel. Once settled, the rest of the day is free to wander the centro storico — the Trevi Fountain, the Pantheon and the Spanish Steps are all within easy walking distance.
days.taste: An evening in Trastevere for classic Roman cucina povera — cacio e pepe, carbonara and a carafe of house wine.

days.title: Rome
days.desc: A guided walking tour brings Imperial Rome to life — the Colosseum, once host to 50,000 spectators, the Roman Forum and Palatine Hill, seat of ancient emperors. Afternoon free for the Vatican Museums or Sistine Chapel.
days.experience: Standing inside the Colosseum's arena-level galleries, imagining the roar of the crowd nearly two thousand years ago.
days.fallbackIncluded: Rome small-group walking tour of Imperial Rome, 2.5 hrs.

days.title: Rome → Naples
days.desc: Travel south to Naples, a city of striking contrasts — grand Bourbon palaces beside the tangle of the Spanish Quarter, all set against the Bay of Naples and the brooding silhouette of Mount Vesuvius.
days.taste: Naples is the birthplace of pizza — a true Margherita here, cooked in a wood-fired oven, is unmissable.

days.title: Naples
days.desc: A free day to explore Naples' UNESCO-listed historic centre along Spaccanapoli, or venture further afield to the ruins of Pompeii, frozen in time by Vesuvius' eruption in 79 AD, or the cliffside towns of the Amalfi Coast.
days.fallbackIncluded: Day at leisure — optional Pompeii or Amalfi Coast excursion available.

days.title: Naples → Florence
days.desc: Head north into Tuscany, arriving in Florence by early evening. Cross the Ponte Vecchio at dusk and take in the Duomo, its terracotta-tiled dome still dominating the skyline as it did in Brunelleschi's day.
days.taste: A first taste of Tuscan cuisine — ribollita or pappa al pomodoro paired with a glass of Chianti.

days.title: Florence
days.desc: A full day among Florence's Renaissance treasures — the Uffizi Gallery's masterworks, Michelangelo's David, and the Boboli Gardens behind the Pitti Palace.
days.shopping: The San Lorenzo market and the boutiques of Via de' Tornabuoni for Florentine leather goods.
days.taste: Bistecca alla Fiorentina, the city's celebrated T-bone steak, served simply grilled.

days.title: Florence → Venice
days.desc: Travel north across the Apennines and the Po Valley to the Venice area. Your hotel is in Mestre, the mainland gateway to Venice, just minutes by train or vaporetto from the lagoon city itself.
days.taste: Cicchetti and a glass of Prosecco at a Venetian bacaro, the local take on tapas.

days.title: Venice
days.desc: A day in Venice itself — St Mark's Square and Basilica, the Doge's Palace and the Bridge of Sighs, then a wander over the Rialto Bridge through the winding calli and canals.
days.experience: A private guided walking tour through Venice's historic sestieri, away from the busiest routes.

days.title: Venice → Milan
days.desc: Travel west across the Veneto and Lombardy plains to Milan, Italy's dynamic capital of fashion, finance and design.
days.taste: An aperitivo in the Navigli district, Milan's canal-side answer to Venice.

days.title: Milan
days.desc: Explore Milan's Gothic Duomo, one of the largest cathedrals in the world, and the elegant Galleria Vittorio Emanuele II. See Sforza Castle and, with advance booking, Leonardo da Vinci's Last Supper.
days.shopping: The Quadrilatero della Moda, Milan's designer fashion district around Via Montenapoleone.
days.fallbackIncluded: Day at leisure — optional Last Supper visit available (advance booking required).

days.title: Depart Milan
days.desc: Free time until your private transfer to Milan airport, depending on flight time.

about.title: Best season
about.body: April–June and September–October offer the mildest weather and lighter crowds across Rome, Florence and Venice.

about.title: Weather
about.body: Warm Mediterranean summers, 25–33°C, especially in Rome and Naples; cooler, often damp winters, particularly around Venice and Milan.

about.title: Currency & docs
about.body: Euro (€) throughout Italy; standard Schengen entry rules apply for non-EU passport holders.

goodToKnow.title: Tickets & timing
goodToKnow.body: Book the Uffizi Gallery and Doge's Palace ahead in peak season — both routinely sell out days in advance. Visit St Mark's Square before 10:00 to avoid the crowds.

goodToKnow.title: Rail travel
goodToKnow.body: Seat reservations are included on all inter-city trains; arrive at least 20 minutes before departure as Italian stations can be large and busy.

goodToKnow.title: Your vehicle
goodToKnow.body: Driver working hours are limited by EU law to around 9 hours per day, so day-trip timings are planned around this. Luggage space fits one large case per guest.

goodToKnow.title: What to pack
goodToKnow.body: Comfortable shoes for cobblestones in Rome, Florence and Venice, plus a light rain layer for Venice and Milan in shoulder season.

goodToKnow.title: Money
goodToKnow.body: Cards are accepted almost everywhere; keep some cash for smaller trattorias and markets. A 10% tip is appreciated in restaurants, though not obligatory.

goodToKnow.title: City taxes
goodToKnow.body: Most Italian cities levy a small nightly city tax, payable locally at the hotel and not included in the package price.

terms: All rates are net and per person for the package.
Child rates apply for children aged 2 to 11 years old. One child sharing a room with 2 adults, subject to hotel policy.
All rates are subject to availability at the time of booking. Europe Incoming will endeavour to match the rates quoted.
Rates are not applicable during trade fair periods, major European public holidays, and other major events.
City taxes are not included in the price.
If certain attractions are closed during specific periods, alternative options will be arranged.
All bookings must be confirmed at least 60 working days prior to arrival.
100% pre-payment required by bank transfer or credit card (Visa or Mastercard). All bank transfer charges covered by the Agent.
Vouchers will be issued after receipt of full payment.
